analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every projection GEMM is the cuBLAS FP32 SIMT kernel 'Kernel2' with tensor cores completely idle. The model runs in pure float32 (torch.randn default, no autocast), so cuBLAS routes mm to the FP32 FMA/SIMT path instead of the tensor-core HGEMM path. The kernel is neither compute-bound nor memory-bound at the SIMT level (sm_throughput ~36%, dram_throughput ~7.5%, eligible_cycles ~58%); it is simply running on units that deliver a small fraction of the device's matmul throughput. Each [4096,512]x[512,512] projection takes ~74,800ns. The grid is [64,1,2] = 128 CTAs, which does not even fill one wave on this ~188-SM Blackwell part (Waves/SM = ceil(128/188) = 1, ~68% of a single wave occupied), so the SIMT kernel also leaves most SMs idle.

transformation.description: Insert dtype casts so the projection GEMMs route to tensor-core HGEMM. Cast the operands of each torch.ops.aten.mm.default node to bfloat16 before the mm and cast the result back to float32 for downstream consumers (LayerNorm / residual). On Blackwell this moves the matmul off the FP32 SIMT path onto the tensor-core HGEMM path, which has roughly an order of magnitude higher matmul throughput. A lighter-weight alternative that avoids any numeric change to the graph is to enable TF32 (torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True), which lets the FP32 inputs use the TF32 tensor-core path with no FX surgery; prefer bf16 casts when accuracy budget allows because TF32 has lower throughput than bf16 HGEMM. This pass is the root enabler: it must run before any pass that registers a fused/pre-transposed weight buffer, because such buffers are allocated at the runtime dtype.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass (Aten IR), per torch 2.11 injection note in memory
fx_steps: out_fp32.args = (node,)  # restore the self-edge broken by replace_all_uses_with
transformation.code_hint: Register via torch._inductor.config.post_grad_custom_pre_pass. Guard the result-cast self-edge as shown so replace_all_uses_with does not rewrite the cast's own input. If only TF32 is desired, skip the FX pass and set torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True before compile.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Weights and activations read as bf16 halve GEMM operand traffic; primary win is throughput (tensor-core HGEMM), estimated ~70% per-GEMM time reduction vs FP32 SIMT. Absolute ns are ncu-inflated 2-5x; treat reduction as relative.

optimizations.notes: Mechanism is unambiguous: tensor_core_active_pct=0 and smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active=0 on every Kernel2 launch prove FP32 SIMT dispatch. bf16 changes numerics slightly; if the workload requires FP32 accuracy use the TF32 flag variant. Estimate covers the 12 attributed Kernel2 launches; the prologue NVTX range also contains Kernel2 launches that double-count the measured ops (see global_notes), so realized wall-clock gain tracks the unique GEMM count, not the inflated kernel total.

bottleneck.description: The q_proj, k_proj and v_proj are three independent torch.ops.aten.mm.default nodes that share the same LHS (the ln_pre output, [4096,512]) and each multiply a distinct [512,512] weight. They launch as three separate Kernel2 GEMMs (~74,800ns each, ~224,400ns total) with identical small grids of [64,1,2]=128 CTAs. With ~188 SMs each GEMM occupies under one wave, so three serial sub-wave launches waste both launch overhead and SM occupancy, and the shared LHS activation is re-read from L2/DRAM three times.

evidence.kernel: Kernel2 (cuBLAS FP32 SIMT GEMM, q/k/v projections)

transformation.description: Concatenate the q/k/v weight tensors along the output dimension into a single [512,1536] weight buffer and replace the three aten.mm.default nodes with one aten.mm.default producing [4096,1536], then split the result with three aten.slice.Tensor nodes feeding the existing view/transpose chains. This collapses three sub-wave kernel launches into one larger GEMM (grid grows ~3x toward filling a full wave), reads the shared LHS activation once, and amortizes launch overhead. Must run after OPT-1: the concatenated weight is materialized via register_buffer at the runtime dtype, so dtype promotion must already have set bf16 (otherwise the buffer is allocated FP32 and cannot be recast).
fx_steps: Identify the three aten.mm.default nodes whose first arg is the same ln_pre output node and whose second args are the q/k/v weight get_attr nodes.
Create a fused weight: torch.cat([Wq, Wk, Wv], dim=1) -> register on the module as a buffer, expose as a get_attr node 'qkv_weight'.
transformation.code_hint: If OPT-1 emitted per-mm bf16 casts on the LHS, dedupe them so the shared LHS is cast once before the fused mm. Pre-concatenate weights at buffer-registration time, not per forward.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Shared LHS activation ([4096,512]) read once instead of three times; better wave utilization on the larger [4096,512]x[512,1536] GEMM. Estimate is the launch+wave savings net of the larger single GEMM, applied per attention block.

optimizations.notes: Estimate is per-block; the profile shows q/k/v as op_id 4/5/6 in one block. Realized gain scales with block count. Confidence is medium because the exact get_attr wiring of the q/k/v weights depends on how Inductor names the parameters post-grad; the transform is a well-established QKV fusion pattern.

bottleneck.description: SDPA lowered to the FP32 CUTLASS memory-efficient attention kernel 'fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80'. Because q/k/v are float32, the SDPA dispatcher cannot select the FlashAttention backend (Flash requires fp16/bf16), so it falls back to the FP32 CUTLASS kernel built for sm80 (Ampere), running on a Blackwell GPU. The kernel spills heavily: l1tex__t_output_wavefronts_pipe_lsu_mem_local = 757,760 local-memory wavefronts with 168 registers/thread, and achieved_occupancy is only 14%. eligible_cycles ~34% indicates the SMs frequently have no warp eligible to issue. Each call costs ~137,400ns, the single most expensive kernel in the profile.

transformation.description: Once q/k/v are bf16 (via OPT-1 propagating into the projection outputs, plus OPT-2's fused projection emitting bf16), the SDPA backend selector dispatches to the FlashAttention kernel instead of the FP32 CUTLASS sm80 fallback. At the Aten IR the relevant node is torch.ops.aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default (Inductor's lowering of F.scaled_dot_product_attention, op_id 7/29); ensure its q/k/v inputs arrive in bf16 and that no intervening _to_copy upcasts them back to fp32 before the SDPA node. With bf16 inputs Flash uses Blackwell tensor cores fully, keeps the softmax/scores in shared memory/registers without the FP32 register pressure, and eliminates the 757k local-memory spills, raising occupancy. Per the structural DAG, SDPA backend selection must come after QKV fusion so the attention pattern (single fused QKV feeding the SDPA node) is fully formed when the pass walks the graph; and after dtype promotion so the inputs are bf16.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass (Aten IR), after OPT-1 and OPT-2
fx_steps: For each of n.args[0:3] (q,k,v): trace the producer; if it is an aten._to_copy.default to float32, rewire to consume the bf16 source so SDPA sees bf16 q/k/v.
Verify is_causal/scale kwargs are preserved on the node (no scale override needed; default scale = 1/sqrt(head_dim=64)).

Insert a single aten._to_copy.default(out, dtype=float32) after n only if the downstream out_proj path was left in fp32 by OPT-1; otherwise keep the bf16 output flowing into the (now bf16) out_proj mm.
transformation.code_hint: Do not hand-write a Flash kernel; let the existing SDPA lowering pick the Flash backend by ensuring bf16 inputs. The win comes from backend dispatch, not from replacing the operator. Confirm post-compile that the emitted kernel name changes away from fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_*.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates 757,760 local-memory spill wavefronts and the FP32 register-pressure path; bf16 Flash kernel estimated ~70% faster than the FP32 CUTLASS fallback. Absolute ns ncu-inflated; relative comparison only.

optimizations.notes: Mechanism (FP32 -> Flash via bf16) is high-confidence; rated medium because the realized backend selection depends on the SDPA dispatcher accepting the bf16 head_dim=64 shapes on this Blackwell driver/torch 2.11 build, and on no upcast sneaking back in before the SDPA node. The 'sm80' suffix on the current kernel confirms an Ampere-targeted FP32 fallback is running on Blackwell, leaving substantial headroom.

global_notes: 0 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0% of attributed time); all 13 operators with non-null aggregated metrics are >= 1%.

knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in the repo. Per documented fallback, FX surgery APIs and hardware specs were sourced from model knowledge: assumed NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell ~ 188 SMs (GB202 class) for Waves/SM, and warp_cycles_per_instruction is null throughout (consistent with its removal on Blackwell), so eligible_cycles_pct was used as the latency-bound indicator.

capture_metadata.measure_iters is not present in this profile, so the warm_up_inflation flag could not be evaluated by the kernel_count-divisibility rule; it was treated as not detected. The 9 init kernels in warnings[] were already excluded from attribution.

fused_kernel_double_count flag set: operators op_id=14 and op_id=36 have is_fused=true (aten::_efficient_attention_forward fused with aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention). Their kernel time is the same fmha launch counted under both the efficient-attention and the SDPA lowering names; do not sum them as independent work.

Layer-dedup double-count caveat: the 'layer::unique::prologue' NVTX range (41% of attributed time, 16 kernels k_00009-k_00029) overlaps the standalone high-confidence torch_profiler ops (aten::mm_0, aten::_efficient_attention_forward_0). The prologue wraps a full-block pass while individual aten ops are also attributed for the measured iterations, so total_attributed_ns (2.22ms) over-counts real wall time. Per-operator pct is reported as-is for ranking, but realized speedups should be scored against the unique GEMM/attention kernel counts, not the inflated totals.

All kernels run on stream_id=7 (no multi_stream_overlap); kernel-name duration CV is <5% (no dynamic_shapes); 4/35 kernels unattributed = 11% (below the 0.3 high_unattributed threshold); timestamps are well-formed (no clock_domain issue).

Root-cause summary: the entire workload runs in float32, which forces (a) every projection GEMM onto the cuBLAS FP32 SIMT path with tensor cores at 0%, and (b) SDPA onto the FP32 CUTLASS sm80 fallback instead of FlashAttention. OPT-1 (dtype promotion / TF32) is the single highest-leverage change and the prerequisite for OPT-2 and OPT-3.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 -> OPT-2 -> OPT-3 (linear chain, no cycles). OPT-2 and OPT-3 both register/derive buffers at runtime dtype so they must follow OPT-1; OPT-3 (SDPA backend selection) must follow OPT-2 (QKV fusion) per the structural constraint that SDPA passes walk the graph after the QKV output nodes exist.

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were not invoked in this environment; novel/uncertain transforms were capped at medium confidence accordingly. FX API usage follows current torch.fx.Graph/Node surgery conventions (inserting_before/after, replace_all_uses_with, erase_node, lint, recompile).
